title: Historical Comparative Schedule Analytics Read Surface And Host Validation
historical_note: This packet records one bounded PM UI/read-surface execution slice from before the canonical repo boundary moved to `C:/APEX Platform/apex-power-ops-platform` on 2026-05-07. It remains packet-history provenance, not a live execution instruction for current repo operations.
current_routing: use PROJECT_STATUS.md for the current residue-retirement lane and latest completed packets
use docs/architecture/OLARES-PUBLICATION-BOUNDARY-RETIREMENT-DEPENDENCY-INVENTORY-2026-05-06.md for the remaining post-cutover boundary closeout queue
use this packet JSON only when historical provenance is needed for the earlier `pm-schema-ui-002g` UI/read-surface execution slice
objective: Implement the smallest bounded follow-on after the sandbox-complete driver and tracer surfaces by adding a read-only comparative schedule analytics surface that exposes current-versus-baseline and snapshot-style variance metrics for imported schedule tasks through the existing GET-only bridge and PM review UI, without adding schedule writes, synthetic baseline fabrication, or a coarse delta model derived from degraded third-party exports.
active_role: PM UI Comparative Schedule Analytics Architect
dependency_note: UI-002g is no longer blocked by the 020f-era substrate findings. Packets 020e.1, 020g-a, 020e.2, and 020h have landed the truthful sandbox substrate, and UI-002e plus UI-002f are now sandbox-complete on top of it. Delegate UI-002g against that landed sandbox substrate. If a true host-browser session is not available in this execution context, host validation should be deferred truthfully rather than fabricated.
constrained_outputs: One bounded GET-only analytics read surface exposing comparative task metrics such as baseline variance, completion state, and selected float/history fields already grounded in imported schedule data
One minimal PM review surface for sorting or filtering tasks by comparative schedule variance without introducing schedule-write behavior

Focused test evidence proving the analytics surface works end to end without depending on the degraded third-party delta-export shape, plus host-browser validation evidence when a real host session is available or an explicit sandbox-complete host-deferred disposition when it is not
validation_steps: Confirm the analytics surface remains GET-only and read-only by construction
Confirm all comparative metrics are derived from persisted schedule fields rather than client-side fabrication or degraded third-party delta exports
Confirm no schedule-write behavior, transform-builder mutation, or billing/class-D behavior is introduced

Confirm host-side browser validation succeeds for representative schedule variance cases when a real host session is available; otherwise close truthfully as sandbox-complete with host validation deferred
sql_permission_note: Packet UI-002g authorizes only bounded GET-only bridge/query and read-only PM UI analytics work over existing persisted schedule data. No new SQL, no schedule-write behavior, and no client-side fabrication are authorized.
